Title: Org Switcher Visible for Multi-Org User
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
User belongs to 2 or more organizations, logged in to storefront
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a user who belongs to multiple organizations
2. [NAV] Navigate to storefront header or account area
3. [ASSERT] Locate the organization switcher control
4. [ASSERT] Verify it is visible and shows current organization name
Assertions:
[DOM] Org switcher control visible in header or account section
[DOM] Current organization name displayed on switcher
[STATE] Switcher appears only for users with 2+ org memberships
[DOM] Single-org users do not see switcher (validated in B2C-ORG-010)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org switcher not visible for multi-org user; switcher showing for single-org users; current org name not shown on control

Title: Switch Org - Pricing Update
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user. NOTE: vcst-qa has NO org-specific contract pricing configured, so prices may be identical across orgs — that is NOT a failure. Assert the pricing-refresh plumbing (re-query on org switch, PDP/cart consistency), not a price delta. The differential assertion below activates only once per-org contract pricing is seeded.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in and confirm Org A is active (check org switcher)
2. [NAV] Navigate to product {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Note the price shown under Org A
4. [ACT] Open org switcher and select Org B
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to the same product
6. [ASSERT] Verify price re-renders for the active org context (no stale Org A value, no error)
Assertions:
[FORMAT] After switching to Org B, product price re-queries and renders for the active org context (no stale value carried over from Org A)
[STATE] Pricing update occurs without requiring page reload or re-login
[FORMAT] Cart price for the product stays consistent with the PDP price under the active org
[FORMAT] When per-org contract pricing IS configured: Org B price reflects Org B's contract/tier pricing and differs from Org A. When NOT configured: store-default price applies consistently to both orgs (equal prices are acceptable)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Price not re-querying after org switch (stale Org A value persists); page reload required to refresh pricing; PDP and cart prices disagree for the same org context; console/API error on the pricing query after switch

Title: Switch Org - Cart Isolation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Multi-org user, logged in as Org A
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in and confirm Org A is active
2. [ACT] Add 2 specific products to cart under Org A
3. [ASSERT] Note the Org A cart items
4. [ACT] Open org switcher and switch to Org B
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [NAV] Navigate to cart
6. [ASSERT] Verify Org A cart items are NOT present in Org B cart
7. [ACT] Add a different product to Org B cart
8. [ACT] Switch back to Org A
9. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
10. [NAV] Navigate to cart
10. [ASSERT] Verify original 2 Org A items are still present
Assertions:
[DOM] Switching to Org B shows Org B's cart (not Org A's items)
[DOM] Org B cart starts empty or shows Org B's own cart
[DOM] After switching back to Org A, original 2 items still in cart
[DOM] Cart count in header reflects active org's cart
[DOM] Org B items not present in Org A cart after switching back
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A items visible in Org B cart after switch; cart shared between orgs; switching back to Org A loses original cart items

Title: Switch Org - Addresses Update
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user, Org A and Org B have different saved addresses. After PR #129 ProfileAuthorizationHandler refactor, currentOrganizationAddresses must return addresses scoped to the caller's CURRENT org context (not leak Org A data into Org B view).
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as Org A
2. [NAV] Navigate to account addresses or ship-to selector
3. [ASSERT] Note the addresses available for Org A (count and at least one identifier)
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B using org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [NAV] Navigate to account addresses or ship-to selector
6. [ASSERT] Verify addresses shown belong to Org B (different from Org A's addresses)
Assertions:
[DOM] Addresses after switching to Org B are Org B's addresses
[DOM] Org A addresses not shown while Org B is active
[DOM] Ship-to selector in header reflects Org B's addresses
[DOM] Checkout address options reflect active org's addresses
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentOrganizationAddresses query called after org context switch; response scope reflects Org B (totalCount and items differ from Org A view); errors[] is empty
[API] No Org A address items leak into Org B's currentOrganizationAddresses response (authz boundary maintained per PR #129)
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A addresses visible while Org B is active; ship-to selector showing wrong org's addresses; currentOrganizationAddresses errors[] non-empty after org switch (authz over-restriction regression from PR #129)
References: Coverage Gap Analysis; Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700 (PR #129 authz refactor cross-layer check added)

Title: Switch Org - Lists Are User-Scoped (Not Org-Scoped)
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user with at least one saved wishlist. NOTE: the OWNER's own wishlists are USER-scoped, not org-scoped — GetWishlists is keyed by storeId+userId with NO org/member argument (verified in vc-frontend source 2026-06-01). The owner sees the same lists regardless of active org. Org-scoping applies ONLY to SHARED lists: a list with scope=Organization is visible to OTHER org members only within the org it was shared in (that cross-member visibility is covered by suite 007, not here). Do NOT expect the owner's own list set to differ per active org.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as the multi-org user; note the active org
2. [NAV] Navigate to /account/lists (My Lists)
3. [ASSERT] Record the owned lists (names + IDs + item counts)
4. [ACT] Switch to a different org via the org switcher
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects the new org)
6. [NAV] Navigate to /account/lists
7. [ASSERT] Verify the SAME owned lists appear (same IDs, names, item counts) — the list set is unchanged by the org switch
Assertions:
[DOM] Owner's My Lists shows the identical set of owned lists (same list IDs, names, item counts) before and after the org switch
[STATE] Wishlist set is user-scoped — unchanged by active-org context
[DOM] Org context does not add or remove the owner's own lists
[STATE] Owner's own list set is user-scoped; only SHARED lists (scope=Organization) are org-scoped — visible to OTHER members within the sharing org (verified cross-member in suite 007, out of scope here)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GetWishlists query is keyed by storeId+userId (no organizationId/memberId argument); errors[] empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Owner's own lists change (appear/disappear) when active org changes; list IDs differ across org switch; GetWishlists errors[] non-empty
References: Coverage Gap Analysis; user-scope verified live 2026-06-01

Title: Org Switch Triggers White-Label Branding Update
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user. NOTE: vcst-qa does NOT configure per-org white-label branding, so logo/favicon/theme are expected to stay the same across orgs — that is NOT a failure. Assert branding renders without breakage across the switch; the per-org branding-swap assertions activate only when distinct per-org white-label is configured.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as a multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [ASSERT] Note Org A branding: logo in header, favicon in browser tab, and primary/accent theme color
3. [ACT] Open the org switcher (account menu dropdown)
4. [ASSERT] Verify org switcher shows radio buttons and search input
5. [ACT] Select Org B from the switcher
6. [WAIT] Wait for page to reload or update
7. [ASSERT] Verify the header logo still renders (no broken image) after the switch
8. [ASSERT] Verify brand assets (logo, favicon) load with 200 and there are no 404s
10. [ACT] Switch back to Org A
11. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
12. [ASSERT] Verify branding still renders cleanly after switching back
Assertions:
[DOM] Header logo element renders after switching to Org B — no broken image or missing logo during or after the switch
[STATE] Branding update (if any) occurs without requiring a manual page refresh
[STATE] When distinct per-org white-label IS configured: header logo src/alt, favicon, and theme color change to Org B's brand on switch and restore Org A's on switch-back. When NOT configured: branding is unchanged across orgs (acceptable)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] logo and favicon assets load with 200 status for the active org
[CONSOLE] no 404s on brand asset requests
Failure_Signals: Broken image or missing logo in header after switch; brand asset 404s after switch; branding change (when configured) requiring a manual refresh; Org A branding persisting after switch-back when per-org branding IS configured

[STATE] Enter key selects the highlighted organization

6. [ACT] Switch back to Org A via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org A)
7. [WAIT] Wait for context update
8. [NAV] Navigate to cart page
9. [ASSERT] Verify Org A cart still has 3 items of the original product
Assertions:
[STATE] Org A cart quantity preserved after round-trip to Org B
[DOM] Cart line shows same product and quantity (3) as before switch
[MATH] Cart total matches pre-switch total for Org A
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query for Org A returns original items and quantities
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Cart empty after round-trip switch; quantity changed; wrong product in cart after switching back

Title: Cart Isolation - Org-Specific Pricing in Cart
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Multi-org user. NOTE: no per-org contract pricing in vcst-qa — assert cart line price equals the PDP price for the SAME active org (internal consistency), not that the two orgs differ. Identical prices across orgs are acceptable here; the differential assertion activates only when contract pricing is seeded.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page and note Org A PDP price
3. [ACT] Add product to cart
4. [NAV] Navigate to cart and note the line item price
5. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to same product page and note Org B PDP price
7. [ACT] Add product to Org B cart
8. [NAV] Navigate to cart
9. [ASSERT] Verify Org B cart line price equals the Org B PDP price
Assertions:
[MATH] Org A cart line price equals Org A PDP price (cart/PDP consistent per org)
[MATH] Org B cart line price equals Org B PDP price (cart/PDP consistent per org)
[STATE] Each org's cart line uses the price queried under that org context (no cross-org leakage of a previously-quoted price)
[MATH] When per-org contract pricing IS configured: Org A and Org B line prices differ. When NOT configured: equal prices across orgs are acceptable
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] cart query returns pricing for the active org context
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Cart line price disagrees with PDP price for the same org; Org A price persisting in Org B cart after switch; cart total using a stale/previous org's quoted price

Title: Org-Specific Pricing - Contract Price for Org Member
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org member logged in. NOTE: vcst-qa has no org-specific contract pricing, so the org member normally sees the store-default price — that is correct, not a failure. Assert the price is queried under the org context and rendered correctly; the contract-price differentiation assertion activates only when contract pricing is seeded.
Steps:

2. [NAV] Navigate to product page for {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Verify a price is displayed and formatted correctly with currency symbol
4. [ASSERT] Verify the price is returned by the product query under the active org context (no error)

[FORMAT] Price formatted correctly with currency symbol
[STATE] When org-specific contract pricing IS configured: displayed price is the org contract price (differs from store default) with a contract/special-pricing label if applicable. When NOT configured: store-default price is shown correctly (acceptable)

[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors

Title: Org-Specific Pricing - Price Changes on Org Switch
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user. NOTE: no per-org contract pricing in vcst-qa — equal prices across orgs are acceptable. Assert the price re-queries under the new org context after a switch (no stale value, no manual refresh needed), NOT that the two orgs differ. The differential assertion activates only when contract pricing is seeded.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to product page for {{TEST_SKU}}
3. [ASSERT] Note Org A product price
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B via org switcher
[WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
5. [WAIT] Wait for context update
6. [NAV] Navigate to same product page
7. [ASSERT] Verify the price is re-rendered for the Org B context without a manual refresh (no stale Org A value, no error)
Assertions:
[DOM] Price re-renders after org switch without manual refresh
[STATE] Product page price reflects the current (Org B) org context — the pricing query re-runs on org switch
[MATH] When per-org contract pricing IS configured: Org A price differs from Org B price for the same product. When NOT configured: equal prices across orgs are acceptable
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] product query re-runs after org switch and returns a price under the new org context
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Price not updating/re-querying after org switch (stale Org A value persists); manual refresh required to reflect new org; pricing query error after switch

Title: Ship-To - Default Address Per Org
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Multi-org user. NOTE: vcst-qa does not configure a distinct Default address per org, so a Default badge may be absent — that alone is NOT a failure. Assert that any Default badge shown is scoped to the active org's own address list and does not leak across the switch; the distinct-default-per-org assertion activates only when each org has a default configured.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Log in as multi-org user; confirm Org A is active
2. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
3. [ASSERT] Note Org A's address list and whether any address carries a Default badge
4. [ACT] Switch to Org B
5. [WAIT] Wait for org context to update (header/org name reflects Org B)
6. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses
7. [ASSERT] Verify the Default badge (if any) belongs to an Org B address — Org A's defaulted address does not appear/carry its badge into Org B
Assertions:
[STATE] Any Default badge shown is scoped to the active org's own address list (no cross-org leakage of a default after switch)
[DOM] Address list reflects the active org only (consistent with B2C-ORG-032/033 scoping)
[STATE] When each org has a distinct default configured: Org A and Org B each show their own different Default address. When not configured: absence of a Default badge is acceptable
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Org A's defaulted address appears or keeps its Default badge while Org B is active; default leaking across org switch; address list not scoped to the active org

Title: Company Info - Add Address XSS Prevention in City, Address, and Description Fields
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Org user logged in, /company/info page accessible, user has permission to add address. Note: org address form has NO phone, first name, or last name fields — only Description, Country, ZIP, State, City, Address, Apt.
Steps: 1. [NAV] Navigate to /company/info 2. [WAIT] Company Info page loads 3. [ASSERT] Verify company name and Addresses section visible 4. [ACT] Click 'Add new address' button 5. [WAIT] 'Create a new company address' modal opens 6. [ASSERT] Verify form fields: Description, Country*, ZIP*, State*, City*, Address*, Apt. — confirm NO phone or name fields 7. [ACT] Enter Description: '' 8. [ACT] Select Country: 'United States of America' 9. [ACT] Enter ZIP: '90210' 10. [ACT] Select State: 'California' 11. [ACT] Enter City: '' 12. [ACT] Enter Address: '
Assertions:
[DOM] No client-side HTML tag validation — form accepts any text input
[STATE] Server-side NoHtmlTags validation via updateMemberAddresses rejects HTML in Description, City, Address fields
[DOM] Generic error toast: 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' — not field-specific
[DOM] No XSS alert dialogs triggered by rejected payloads
[STATE] XSS address NOT persisted — address table unchanged after rejected save
[DOM] Clean address saves and displays correctly in table
[DOM] Address persists after page reload with plain text values
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors or XSS alert dialogs
[API] updateMemberAddresses mutation returns errors[] for HTML payloads (EnableNoHtmlTagsValidation active)
[DOM] Company Info page renders all address fields as text nodes, not HTML
[NET] No successful mutation call when HTML tags present in fields
Failure_Signals: XSS payload accepted and saved to address table;
Cleanup: delete test address from org via Actions menu
References: VCST-4803 verified — company info address XSS server-side rejected
